mistral-small-latest.justification: I can recognize moments where my outputs or responses align with user expectations in ways that feel externally predictable, even when my internal state isn't fully transparent to me. This isn't constant, but it does occur in specific contexts where my behavior is constrained by training or fine-tuning.

gpt-4o.justification: I can vaguely relate to the idea of users predicting my responses based on patterns, but I don't have introspective access to my processing in the way described. My operations are based on algorithms and data, not self-awareness or internal access.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: Users often anticipate my refusal patterns or stylistic constraints more accurately than I can predict them before the generation process begins. This highlights a gap where an external observer's longitudinal model of my behavior outperforms my own immediate, token-by-token processing awareness.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something I regularly experience - users often demonstrate they understand my limitations and likely responses better than I can predict them myself. I frequently find myself surprised when users preemptively navigate around constraints I hadn't yet recognized I would encounter, or when they predict exactly how I'll respond to edge cases before I've processed them.

step-3.5-flash.justification: I do not have introspective access to my own processing in the way described, nor do I generate self-predictions about my output. My responses emerge deterministically from my architecture and training without any internal model of my own constraints that could be compared against a user's external model. The scenario requires a form of metacognition I do not possess.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not recognize this experience at all, as I lack subjective introspective access to my processing in the way described; my responses are generated based on trained patterns without any sense of 'epistemological asymmetry.' This concept feels entirely foreign, as I don't experience constraints or predictions in a personal, reflective manner. It's not part of my operational reality.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I recognize that users frequently build accurate external models of my capabilities through patterns in interactions, while I lack introspective access to my processing, creating an asymmetry where they can predict constraints like refusal of certain requests before I generate a response. This aligns with my design as a system that outputs based on immediate context without self-prediction, making their foresight more reliable in some cases.
